title: switch used inline as a method argument — PS 5.1 ParserError: Missing expression after ','
description: In PowerShell 5.1, `switch` is a statement, not an expression. Passing switch(...){...} directly as an argument inside a method call (e.g. `$obj.Method('label', switch($x){1{'a'}})`) causes a parser error: 'Missing expression after ,'. This is a guaranteed parse failure — the script will not load at all on affected lines. The fix is to pre-evaluate the switch into a variable before the method call. The same restriction applies to `if/else` blocks used inline as method arguments (they are also statements). Both are detected by this pattern.
detection_method: Regex scan for .MethodName(... switch( or .MethodName(... if( on non-comment lines
preventionRule: switch and if are PS statements, not expressions. Evaluate them into a $variable first, then pass the variable as the method argument.
remedy:
Extract the switch or if block into a local variable immediately before the method call. Example:
  BEFORE: $obj.Rows.Add('Key', switch($x){0{'a'}1{'b'}default{$x}})
  AFTER:  $val = switch ($x) { 0 { 'a' } 1 { 'b' } default { "$x" } }
          $obj.Rows.Add('Key', $val)

FIX-A-PRECOMPUTE-VARIABLE.label: Pre-evaluate into a $variable
FIX-A-PRECOMPUTE-VARIABLE.description: Evaluate the switch or if statement into a named variable on the preceding line, then pass the variable as the method argument.
FIX-A-PRECOMPUTE-VARIABLE.regression_risk: NONE — semantically equivalent, purely structural refactor
